title: Crop Yield Gap Analysis For Smallholder Farmers In Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring the gap between actual and attainable crop yields for smallholder farmers, linking satellite-derived yield estimates, soil data, and climate data at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: Sub-Saharan African cereal yields average 1.5 tons per hectare versus 3+ tons attainable with improved practices. Closing the gap could double food production without expanding farmland. Global Yield Gap Atlas provides modeled estimates, but sub-national verification linking satellite yield estimates, soil constraints, and farmer practice data is underdeveloped.

success_metrics.target: Every yield estimate links satellite or survey data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
